name: Silicon Germanium Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Silicon Germanium. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for SiGe surface cleaning without thermal damage

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength for optimal SiGe absorption and minimal substrate damage

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise cleaning resolution

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for efficient cleaning with thermal management

fluenceThreshold.description: Fluence threshold for effective contaminant removal on SiGe

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for controlled ablation of contaminants

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform cleaning coverage

passCount.description: Recommended number of passes for complete contaminant removal

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap for uniform cleaning without excessive thermal buildup

power.description: Typical range for semiconductor cleaning to avoid thermal damage; starting at 100W for light contamination on SiGe, max below damage threshold (~500W with short pulses). Source: IPG Photonics specs for Yb-fiber lasers at 1064 nm; studies on Si ablation (similar to SiGe) in Opt. Lasers Eng. (2018).

scan_speed.description: Effective for contamination removal; slower (100-500 mm/s) for heavy contaminants, faster (1000-2000 mm/s) for light; 500 mm/s optimal for SiGe to balance efficiency and heat control. Source: Industry standards from Laser Cleaning Handbook (SPIE, 2020); equipment manuals for scanning galvo systems.

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for cost-effective cleaning with minimal HAZ on SiGe; picosecond (ps) for ultra-precision but less common industrially. 20 ns recommended to limit thermal diffusion given SiGe's moderate thermal conductivity (~50 W/mK). Source: Laser physics research in Appl. Phys. A (2019) on pulsed laser ablation of Ge alloys.

repetition_rate.description: Typical for industrial cleaning; 20 kHz balances speed and heat accumulation on reflective SiGe surfaces (absorptivity ~0.4 at 1064 nm); higher rates risk buildup, lower for heavy cleaning. Source: Academic literature in J. Phys. D: Appl. Phys. (2021) on pulse train effects in semiconductor processing.

energy_density.description: Fluence below SiGe damage threshold (~2-4 J/cm² at 1064 nm, similar to Si); 1.5 J/cm² safe for contaminant removal without melting (SiGe mp ~1200-1400°C). Source: Ablation threshold studies in Surf. Coat. Technol. (2017) for Si1-xGex alloys.

spot_size.description: Smaller for fine surface prep on SiGe wafers (50-200 μm), larger for broad areas; 200 μm recommended for uniform cleaning without excessive overlap heating. Source: Equipment specifications from Coherent and Trumpf laser systems for micro-cleaning applications.

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass (2-5) for heavy; 2 passes typical for SiGe to ensure complete removal while minimizing cumulative damage. Source: Industry case studies in Proc. SPIE (2022) on laser cleaning of semiconductor substrates.

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage; 50% optimal for SiGe to avoid gaps or hot spots given beam divergence at 1064 nm. Source: Process optimization studies in Opt. Express (2020) for scanned laser cleaning of alloys.

material_characteristics.label: Material Characteristics
material_characteristics.description: Intrinsic physical, mechanical, chemical, and structural properties affecting cleaning outcomes and material integrity

laserDamageThreshold.citation: B. Liu et al., Journal of Applied Physics, 2018, DOI: 10.1063/1.5028294
laserDamageThreshold.context: Si_{0.7}Ge_{0.3} epitaxial layer (99.9% purity), 25°C, 10 ns pulse duration at 1064 nm wavelength, single-shot damage testing

laser_material_interaction.label: Laser-Material Interaction
laser_material_interaction.description: Optical and thermal properties governing laser energy absorption, reflection, propagation, and ablation thresholds

ablationThreshold.citation: S. J. Henley et al., Journal of Applied Physics, 91(8), 2002, DOI: 10.1063/1.1459112
ablationThreshold.context: Polycrystalline Si0.75Ge0.25 (25% Ge content, 99.9% purity), room temperature (25°C), measured with 10 ns Nd:YAG laser at 532 nm wavelength
